verse_text: Send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey with haste, that they may lack nothing.

links.context: Apollos was a learned Jew from Alexandria, Egypt, "mighty in the Scriptures" (Acts 18:24). Originally a follower of "the baptism of John" ([[ACT.18:25]]), he was taught more fully about Christ by Priscilla and Aquila at Ephesus, and he later preached at Corinth, where some saw him as a rival to Paul. Tradition tells us Apollos became the first bishop of Crete (see [[TIT.3:13]]). Paul makes it plain that he and Apollos are partners in apostolic work and that Apollos would not approve of the factionalism in Corinth (3:4-6; 4:6; 16:12).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
